compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect polyglot lint/format gates
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update CLAUDE.md, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md, and any other affected documentation to accurately reflect the new commands.lint, commands.format, and commands.format_check keys and the stack-agnostic gate pipeline.

**Why**: Future agents need accurate awareness of the gate pipeline's polyglot behavior; CLAUDE.md's architecture section currently describes Python-only linting/formatting, and the new config keys need CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md entries.

**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md architecture section updates; CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md entries for commands.lint, commands.format, commands.format_check; KNOWN-ISSUES.md additions if relevant
- OUT: SKILL.md files (covered by S4); onboarding changes (covered by S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md contains entries for commands.lint, commands.format, and commands.format_check with usage, type, default value, and consumers documented
  ← Satisfies: "Epic validation: polyglot gate pipeline is observable and documented"
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md architecture section accurately describes the stack-agnostic gate pipeline (commands.lint/format/format_check keys, fallback behavior, per-stack onboarding defaults)
  ← Satisfies: "Epic validation: future agents have accurate context"

## Considerations

- Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting, structure, and conventions

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Test
